channel.title: Comments for page "Halloween on 17th Street"
channel.description: Posts in the discussion thread "Halloween on 17th Street"

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Well at least you made a possible connection of all those fan-works of Iris and Able to an item(seriously 4,000 porn and romance material a week sent me for put on AO3/Fanfiction to trash), but I would believe it more if Clef disproved the Witch Child to re-imagine her powers. But I do like the ending and the soon to be art of those kiddie SCP's on this site next Halloween. Keep experimenting :)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is very well thought out and executed. My favourite of the Halloween tales I’ve read so far. Nice work.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: At first I was feeling conflicted about upvoting because it conflicted with a few of my headcanons (and I walked into this thinking it was a mundane AU), but the twist at the end changed it for me. Cute and cosy tale with an appropriately grim ending. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: "Life's no fun without a good scare", indeed. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yeah I know that using mostly series 1 is not so great but they work best for something like this. Series 1 scips are shorter so they can be put in tales relatively easy, and they’re very recognizable which makes them good for something where they aren’t exactly the same character because the reader can still tell who I’m talking about.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Nice, like it.
But:

There were several seconds of tense silence, during which Iris let herself hope he wasn't home after all,

Shouldn’t that be Sigurrós ?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It was so wonderfully heartwarming. I really enjoyed seeing familiar faces in a simpler environment.

And then it wasn't, and I didn't.

+1, you magnificent monster.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
And I thought I couldn’t be made to like something with this many Series 1 skips. Good Job!

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
On a dark Halloween night ten long years ago, (account deleted) created a page called "Scp 239". At the time of its creation, it contained only one line of text: "SCP 239- Witch Child. In Progress". That simple beginning evolved into the Foundation's most notorious Mary Sue, but Sigurrós Stefánsdóttir remains a cornerstone of wiki history. Dr. Clef's attempt to decommission her ushered in the age of lolFoundation, and steered the course of all that came after. But that's not why I wrote a tale about her. I wrote this tale, my entry in the Halloween2018 contest, because of the very first comment on SCP-239, by Lee Byron:

That is holy crap adorable. I want to go trick or treating with her!

Your wish is my command.

+ show block
– hide block

This was called "How Dr. Clef Saved Halloween" when it was in my Sandbox and the Drafts and Critiques forum, in case you don't recognize it.

This follows the "one survivor" rule (in that Clef is the only one unaffected by the VK) and the "Halloween to sunrise" rule.

If you have any questions or something wasn't clear, feel free to post your questions here. I'll answer as best I'm able.

Thanks to Baronjoe for reviewing, btw.
